strengths: The hypothesis was tested on a large sample size of 1,276 trades, providing high statistical confidence in the outcome.
weaknesses: The core hypothesis is conclusively invalidated, with an aggregate Win Rate of only 5.3% and a strongly negative expectancy of -6.78 bps.

Inverting the signal direction from its parent spec (`iter006_deep_book_vs_bbo_divergence`) yielded the exact same abysmal result, indicating that the underlying condition of book divergence is not predictive of price moves in either direction at this horizon.
The signal is consistently and profoundly wrong across all tested symbols, with aggregate per-symbol Win Rates of 7.7% for 005930 and 1.9% for 000660.
recommended_direction_reasoning: The spec's hypothesis is conclusively invalidated, with a 5.3% Win Rate over 1,276 trades. The horizon curve in the backtest data shows WR slightly improving at much longer horizons (from 5.3% at 10 ticks to 25.3% at 200 ticks), suggesting a final exploration for a very long-term mean-reversion effect at the 200-tick horizon is warranted before fully retiring this concept.
